title: elymus pauciflorus subsp. pseudorepens
intro: Elymus pauciflorus subsp. pseudorepens, also known as a type of wild rye or grass, is a plant species that belongs to the Poaceae family. This plant is believed to have medicinal properties, with its roots and stems used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Elymus pauciflorus subsp. pseudorepens include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and act as an expectorant to ease congestion. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to promote overall well-being and relieve symptoms associated with respiratory infections.
